This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

[[ja:十七世神]] [[sn:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[sn:he 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:17 spirits of the world]] [[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tomarinanayo no Kami}} {{DEFAULTSORT:The 17 Generations of Deities}}
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities-->

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft|12=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=draft|12=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。

This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

[[ja:十七世神]] [[sn:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[sn:he 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:17 spirits of the world]] [[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tomarinanayo no Kami}} {{DEFAULTSORT:The 17 Generations of Deities}}
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities-->

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft|12=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=draft|12=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|12=draft|12=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=draft|12=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft|12=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。

This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

[[ja:十七世神]] [[sn:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[sn:he 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:17 spirits of the world]] [[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tomarinanayo no Kami}} {{DEFAULTSORT:The 17 Generations of Deities}}
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities-->

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft|12=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=draft|12=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。

This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Genealogy == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo Clan|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Izumo|ja|古代出雲|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Izumo|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|ja|出雲大社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo Grand Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|12=draft|12=draft}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The 17 Generations of Deities]][[Category:qq]] }}
[[ja:十七世神]] [[sn:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[sn:he 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:17 spirits of the world]] [[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]] [[en:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tomarinanayo no Kami}} {{DEFAULTSORT:The 17 Generations of Deities}}
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> <!--The 17 Generations of Deities-->

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

revision.comment: 15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:十七世神 up to rev 102713580
revision.text:
'''十七世神'''（とおまりななよのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神々]]の総称。
== 概要 == [[大国主神]]に連なる系譜の神々として『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場し、名義未詳、事績不明の神々で構成されている。十七世（とおあまりななよ）はその世代数を指すが、総数は数えても十六柱しか存在せず、何故十七世と形容するのかは不明である。[[イザナギ]]もしくは[[粟鹿大明神元記]]において[[大国主]]十世孫に当たる[[大田田根子]]を含めると十七世となる。
『古事記』以外の文献では、『[[出雲国風土記]]』に[[八束水臣津野命]]が、『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』には[[須佐之男命]]から[[大国主神]]までの系図が掲載されているが、いずれの史料でも鳥鳴海神以降の神が確認できない。
== 考証 == 『古事記』に記された須佐之男命の系譜と大国主神の系譜は、もとは一つの連続した記事だったとされる。[[大年神]]の系譜を含めた三系譜全体の構造が問題となるが、本文の不審や、系譜と物語との内容の食い違いなど、疑問点が多い。
系譜中の神々の多くは他文献に見えず、『古事記』でも事跡が語られないため、それぞれの意義や関係性が明らかにしがたい。全体の解釈としては、大国主神の性格付けが[[天皇]]系譜との対比によって系譜に示されているとする説や、国土神から水の神へとその誕生を物語る出雲土着の神話が骨子になっているとする説、神の祝福を受けて豊かな自然環境が出現することを望む人々の願いが反映されているとする説がある<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
また、この神を含めて大国主神の神裔を指して「十七世神」とされている点について、代替わりを繰り返しており神よりも人に近づいた存在であると指摘する説がある<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[建速須佐之男命]]は[[櫛名田比売]]（[[アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|足名椎]]の娘）を娶り[[八島士奴美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[木花知流比売]]（[[大山津見神]]の娘）を娶り[[布波能母遅久奴須奴神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[日河比売]]（[[淤加美神]]の娘）を娶り[[深淵之水夜礼花神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[天之都度閇知泥神]]を娶り[[淤美豆奴神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[布帝耳神]]（[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘）を娶り[[天之冬衣神]]を産んだ。（なおこの天之冬衣神は天之葺根神という名も持ち{{Sfn|近藤敏喬|1993}}、須佐之男命の子ともいう<ref>日御崎神社社伝。</ref>。子の[[清武豊彦命]]は[[日御崎神社]]神主家[[小野氏 (社家)|小野氏]]の祖{{Sfn|近藤敏喬|1993}}。）
この神は[[刺国若比売]]（[[刺国大神]]の娘）を娶り[[大国主神]]（大穴牟遅神。異母兄弟に[[八十神]]あり。）を産んだ。
この神は[[鳥取神]]（[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘）を娶り[[鳥鳴海神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶り[[国忍富神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[前玉比売]]（[[天之甕主神]]の娘）を娶り[[甕主日子神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[比那良志毘売]]（[[淤加美神]]の娘）を娶り[[多比理岐志麻流美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[活玉前玉比売神]]（[[比比羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘）を娶とり[[美呂浪神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[青沼馬沼押比売]]（[[敷山主神]]の娘）を娶り[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[若尽女神]]を娶り[[天日腹大科度美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[遠津待根神]]（[[天之狭霧神]]の娘）を娶り[[遠津山岬多良斯神]]を産んだ。

{{日本神話}} {{デフォルトソート:とおまりななよのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[十七世神]] to [[The 17 Generations of Deities]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''十七世神'''（とおまりななよのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神々]]の総称。
== 概要 == [[大国主神]]に連なる系譜の神々として『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場し、名義未詳、事績不明の神々で構成されている。十七世（とおあまりななよ）はその世代数を指すが、総数は数えても十六柱しか存在せず、何故十七世と形容するのかは不明である。[[イザナギ]]もしくは[[粟鹿大明神元記]]において[[大国主]]十世孫に当たる[[大田田根子]]を含めると十七世となる。
『古事記』以外の文献では、『[[出雲国風土記]]』に[[八束水臣津野命]]が、『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』には[[須佐之男命]]から[[大国主神]]までの系図が掲載されているが、いずれの史料でも鳥鳴海神以降の神が確認できない。
== 考証 == 『古事記』に記された須佐之男命の系譜と大国主神の系譜は、もとは一つの連続した記事だったとされる。[[大年神]]の系譜を含めた三系譜全体の構造が問題となるが、本文の不審や、系譜と物語との内容の食い違いなど、疑問点が多い。
系譜中の神々の多くは他文献に見えず、『古事記』でも事跡が語られないため、それぞれの意義や関係性が明らかにしがたい。全体の解釈としては、大国主神の性格付けが[[天皇]]系譜との対比によって系譜に示されているとする説や、国土神から水の神へとその誕生を物語る出雲土着の神話が骨子になっているとする説、神の祝福を受けて豊かな自然環境が出現することを望む人々の願いが反映されているとする説がある<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
また、この神を含めて大国主神の神裔を指して「十七世神」とされている点について、代替わりを繰り返しており神よりも人に近づいた存在であると指摘する説がある<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[建速須佐之男命]]は[[櫛名田比売]]（[[アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|足名椎]]の娘）を娶り[[八島士奴美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[木花知流比売]]（[[大山津見神]]の娘）を娶り[[布波能母遅久奴須奴神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[日河比売]]（[[淤加美神]]の娘）を娶り[[深淵之水夜礼花神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[天之都度閇知泥神]]を娶り[[淤美豆奴神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[布帝耳神]]（[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘）を娶り[[天之冬衣神]]を産んだ。（なおこの天之冬衣神は天之葺根神という名も持ち{{Sfn|近藤敏喬|1993}}、須佐之男命の子ともいう<ref>日御崎神社社伝。</ref>。子の[[清武豊彦命]]は[[日御崎神社]]神主家[[小野氏 (社家)|小野氏]]の祖{{Sfn|近藤敏喬|1993}}。）
この神は[[刺国若比売]]（[[刺国大神]]の娘）を娶り[[大国主神]]（大穴牟遅神。異母兄弟に[[八十神]]あり。）を産んだ。
この神は[[鳥取神]]（[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘）を娶り[[鳥鳴海神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶り[[国忍富神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[前玉比売]]（[[天之甕主神]]の娘）を娶り[[甕主日子神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[比那良志毘売]]（[[淤加美神]]の娘）を娶り[[多比理岐志麻流美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[活玉前玉比売神]]（[[比比羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘）を娶とり[[美呂浪神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[青沼馬沼押比売]]（[[敷山主神]]の娘）を娶り[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[若尽女神]]を娶り[[天日腹大科度美神]]を産んだ。
この神は[[遠津待根神]]（[[天之狭霧神]]の娘）を娶り[[遠津山岬多良斯神]]を産んだ。

{{日本神話}} {{デフォルトソート:とおまりななよのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appear exclusively in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Genealogy == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo Clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Izumo|ja|古代出雲|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Izumo|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|ja|出雲大社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo Grand Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
