[[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:十七世神]] {{wikidata link|Q65249569}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十七世神]][[zh:十七世神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65249569]] Wikidata name: 17 spirits of the world [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, appear exclusively in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|qq=}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48745416}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mironami-no-Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=}}.

[[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:十七世神]] {{wikidata link|Q65249569}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十七世神]][[zh:十七世神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65249569]] Wikidata name: 17 spirits of the world [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, appear exclusively in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|qq=}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48745416}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mironami-no-Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

This god married {{ill|Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=}}.

<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:十七世神]] {{wikidata link|Q65249569}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十七世神]][[zh:十七世神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65249569]] Wikidata name: 17 spirits of the world [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, appear exclusively in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|qq=}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48745416}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mironami-no-Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ancient Izumo|ja|古代出雲|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Izumo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Izumo|1=Ancient Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410904}} * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]] * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十七世神]][[zh:十七世神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65249569]] Wikidata name: 17 spirits of the world [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, appear exclusively in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|qq=}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48745416}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}},<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref> Their child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mironami-no-Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ancient Izumo|ja|古代出雲|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Izumo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Izumo|1=Ancient Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410904}} * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]] * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十七世神]][[zh:十七世神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65249569]] Wikidata name: 17 spirits of the world [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, appear exclusively in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|qq=}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48745416}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [[ja:The 17 Generations of Deities]]
<!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--><!--The 17 Generations of Deities--> [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:十七世神]] {{wikidata link|Q65249569}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十七世神]][[zh:十七世神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65249569]] Wikidata name: 17 spirits of the world [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The "17 spirits of the world" (十七世神) is a a group of deities in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Contrary to the name the group does not contain 17 members.
== Overview == These deities, which belong to the genealogy of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, appear exclusively in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], though their meanings and accomplishments are unclear. The term "Seventeen Generations" refers to the number of generations, but only sixteen deities are counted. It's unknown why it's described as seventeen generations. Including {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, who is said to be the tenth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi in either [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] or the {{ill|Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakusa Daijingu Motoki|qq=}}, results in a total of seventeen.
However the ancestry of {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} is established in the first two generations as being [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010"/>{{rp|277}}<ref>{{cite book | last=Frédéric | first=L. | author2=Louis-Frédéric | last3=Roth | first3=K. | title=Japan Encyclopedia | publisher=Belknap Press of Harvard University Press | series=Harvard University Press reference library | year=2005 | isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC | access-date=2020-11-21 }}</ref>{{rp|278}} (Ōnamuchi)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> then [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |title=Kotoshironushi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=28 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}</ref> and then [[Kamo no Okimi]]<ref name="varley-h-paul-1980" /><ref name="神話の中のヒメたち-もうひとつの古事記">『神話の中のヒメたち もうひとつの古事記』p94-97「初代皇后は「神の御子」」</ref>
In other sources, such as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48745416}} appears, while the genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to Ōkuninushi is found in the "Amakusa Daijingu Motoki," but deities following {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} can't be confirmed in any of these materials.
== Verification == It is suggested that the genealogies of Susanoo and Ōkuninushi in the Kojiki were originally one continuous account. The overall structure of the three genealogies, including that of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, is problematic due to discrepancies and the inconsistency between the genealogies and their stories.
Many of the deities in these genealogies are not found in other sources, and their achievements are not mentioned even in the Kojiki, making their significance and relationships difficult to discern. Theories include the genealogy contrasting Ōkuninushi with the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]] lineage, Izumo mythology transitioning from land to water deities, or reflecting people's desire for abundant natural environments to emerge blessed by deities.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース</ref>。
Another theory points out that the "Seventeen Generations Deities" signify the descendants of Ōkuninushi, who repeated generational changes, drawing closer to humans rather than gods.<ref>國學院大学 古事記学センター 神名データベース 天日腹大科度美神</ref>。
== Genealogy == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
